_note: TEMPLATE — canonical NON-SECRET tenant identity for a Hawaii Wing CADETS Apps Script project. Copy the values into TENANT_* Script Properties (or run setupHiwgCadetsScriptProperties() in config-tenants/setup-hiwg.gs). Fields marked FILL_IN must be supplied by Hawaii Wing; the rest are pre-set. Do NOT put these in config.gs — a clasp push overwrites it. Secrets are NOT stored here — see docs/ADMIN_GUIDE.md.
_behavioral_note: TENANT_PROFILE=cadets selects cadet behavior (CADET-only member types, CADET_LITE=true, access/public-contact auto-create off, distribution lists = all-hands + cadets + parents, no senior-side or command-staff lists). No config.gs edits needed.
_label_note: TENANT_WING_ABBREVIATION and TENANT_WING_NAME are left blank on purpose: config.gs derives 'HIWG' and 'Hawaii Wing' from TENANT_WING=HI. The cadet->senior transition-complete email masthead uses TENANT_WING_NAME, so set it here if 'Hawaii Wing' is not the wanted label.
_xt_note: If Hawaii runs cross-tenant contacts (publish the SENIOR roster into this cadet GAL, mirror of the seniors project), set XT_PEER_DOMAIN to the senior Workspace domain and configure the peer service account. Otherwise leave XT_PEER_DOMAIN blank.
_transition_note: TENANT_PROFILE=cadets makes this tenant the transition SOURCE (owns the cadet->senior mail/Drive/contacts migration lifecycle). It reads the peer senior directory to find destination accounts — configure the peer SA the same way as cross-tenant contacts.
_command_domain_note: Unit command staff (commanders, personnel officers, deputy commanders) are SENIOR members and hold no cadet-domain account, so notifications/RecoveryEmailNotify.gs must address them on the SENIOR domain. Required on a cadet tenant; blank would derive addresses on the cadet domain and mail nobody.
